reforms.description: The federal government has proposed a substantial increase in funding of CAD 8.4 billion over the next five years to improve socio-economic conditions facing Indigenous Peoples, with a focus on education and training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in education and training for Indigenous Peoples is expected to enhance human capital, leading to improved economic opportunities and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget includes a significant increase in funding for Indigenous education and infrastructure.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major investment aimed at addressing long-standing socio-economic disparities, with potential long-term impacts on growth and inclusiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The budget also proposes a substantial increase in funding of CAD 8.4 billion over the next five years to improve the sub-standard socio-economic conditions facing Indigenous Peoples.

reforms.description: The federal government is launching a new initiative to help high-impact innovative firms scale up and enhance their global competitiveness by providing tailored services such as finance, advice, and innovation support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to improve the competitiveness of innovative firms, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal budget proposes to launch this initiative in 2016-17.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to support innovative firms, which is crucial for enhancing Canada's economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: The 2016 federal budget proposes to launch a new initiative in 2016-17 to help high-impact (innovative) firms scale up and further their global competitiveness.

reforms.description: The federal government is reviewing the Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) tax credit to ensure it provides value for money and effectively supports R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By evaluating and potentially improving the SR&ED tax credit, the government aims to enhance R&D investment, which can drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government should re-examine the SR&ED tax credit in the context of its announced tax expenditure review.
reforms.importance_rationale: This review is significant as it addresses a major instrument for R&D funding, which is critical for fostering innovation in Canada.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government should re-examine the SR&ED tax credit... to ensure that they are providing value for money.

reforms.description: The 2016 federal budget increases funding for the Industrial Research Assistance Program by CAD 50 million, building on previous increases, to enhance support for business R&D.
reforms.component_name: Industrial Research Assistance Program funding increase
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D is expected to enhance innovation capacity in the private sector, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget allocated additional funding for the program.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding increase is significant but builds on existing support mechanisms, enhancing the overall R&D funding landscape without a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The 2016 federal budget increases funding for the Industrial Research Assistance Program by CAD 50 million.

reforms.description: The federal government is increasing support for innovation networks and clusters with CAD 800 million in new funding to be allocated through the forthcoming Innovation Agenda.
reforms.component_name: Support for innovation networks and clusters
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in innovation networks and clusters is expected to foster collaboration among firms and enhance the commercialization of new technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget made available CAD 800 million for innovation networks and clusters.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding represents a significant investment in fostering innovation ecosystems, which can lead to enhanced economic growth and productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The 2016 budget also makes available CAD 800 million in new support for innovation networks and clusters.

reforms.description: The government is subjecting the Industrial Research Assistance Program and other R&D support programs to rigorous cost-benefit evaluations to improve their effectiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the evaluation of R&D programs is expected to enhance their effectiveness and ensure better allocation of public resources towards innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: A cost-benefit evaluation of IRAP was conducted as part of the program evaluation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the accountability and effectiveness of public R&D funding, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Subject the Industrial Research Assistance Program and other R&D support programs to rigorous cost-benefit evaluations.

reforms.description: The federal government operates several programs to increase lending to small businesses, including the Business Development Bank of Canada's Financing Program and the Small Business Financing Program, which guarantees loans from the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases access to finance for small businesses, enabling them to invest in innovation and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government operates these programs to support small business lending.
reforms.importance_rationale: These programs represent a significant effort to enhance financing options for small businesses, which are crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government also operates a number of programmes to increase lending to small businesses either directly or through the government-owned Business Development Bank of Canada (BDC).

reforms.description: The federal government announced CAD 2 billion for the creation of the Low Carbon Economy Fund, which will support provincial and territorial actions to address climate change, focusing on clean technology and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to foster innovation in clean technology, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government has announced CAD 2 billion for the creation of the Low Carbon Economy Fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant investment aimed at promoting clean technology and innovation, which is crucial for addressing climate change and fostering economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has also recently announced CAD 2 billion for the creation of the Low Carbon Economy Fund, which will support provincial and territorial actions to address climate change.

reforms.description: Canada's small business support programs are recommended to focus more on reducing market failures and supporting start-ups and young firms with innovative projects, which would enhance small business dynamism and productivity growth.
reforms.component_name: Focus on Start-ups and Young Firms
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By redirecting support to innovative start-ups, the initiative aims to boost productivity and resource allocation towards more dynamic firms.
reforms.status_evidence: Recommendations for increasing the contribution of small business programs to productivity were discussed.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to significantly enhance the effectiveness of existing small business support programs, which is crucial for improving overall productivity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Focusing support more on reducing clear market failures would increase the contribution of these programmes to productivity growth and living standards.

reforms.description: The Canadian government operates several programs to support small businesses, including financing, R&D support, and entrepreneurship encouragement, with a focus on addressing market failures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These programs aim to enhance productivity and innovation in the SME sector, which is crucial for overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government continues to operate various programs to support small businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: These programs represent a significant effort to enhance the support structure for small businesses, which are vital for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Canadian governments operate a number of programmes to support small businesses, aimed at facilitating small business financing, supporting research and development (R&D) and innovation, and encouraging entrepreneurship.

reforms.description: The Canadian government should conduct a review of small business support to identify capital market failures and the policy instruments best suited to address them, focusing on innovative projects rather than size alone.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Targeted support for innovative projects can enhance investment in high-growth potential firms, thereby boosting productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Canadian government should conduct a review of small business support.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed review aims to refine existing support mechanisms, potentially leading to more effective interventions for innovative small businesses.
reforms.source_quote: The Canadian government should conduct its own review of small business support to identify capital market failures and the policy instruments best suited to addressing them.

reforms.description: The federal government announced the Venture Capital Action Plan (VCAP) in January 2013 to boost the venture capital industry, involving CAD 400 million in direct and indirect investments focused on later-stage financing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The VCAP aims to attract private investment into the venture capital sector, which can enhance innovation and economic growth through increased funding for startups.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government announced the Venture Capital Action Plan in January 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: The VCAP represents a significant strategic initiative to revitalize the venture capital market in Canada, addressing critical funding gaps for innovative firms.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government announced the Venture Capital Action Plan (VCAP) in January 2013 to boost the venture capital industry.

reforms.description: The BDC's Growth and Transition Capital programme targets high-potential firms needing financing to sustain growth or transition between owners, addressing capital market failures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financing for high-risk projects, this programme helps innovative firms access necessary capital, fostering entrepreneurship and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The BDC's Growth and Transition Capital programme is actively providing financing to high-potential firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This programme is a significant but narrower initiative aimed at addressing specific financing gaps for innovative firms, enhancing their growth potential.
reforms.source_quote: The BDC's Growth and Transition Capital programme targets high-potential firms that need financing to sustain growth or to transition between owners.

reforms.description: The Canadian government proposed to enhance R&D tax credits for small Canadian-controlled private corporations (CCPCs) with qualifying annual R&D expenditures, providing a refundable tax credit of 35% for eligible expenses up to CAD 3 million.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The enhanced tax credits incentivize R&D investment among small firms, addressing market failures related to underinvestment in innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The enhanced tax credits were included in the 2016 budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly increases the financial support for R&D activities among small firms, which is crucial for fostering innovation and competitiveness in the Canadian economy.
reforms.source_quote: Enhanced R&D tax credits for small companies... the enhanced Scientific Research and Experimental Development (SR&ED) refundable tax credit is 35%.
